Echocardiography shows small vegetations on the aortic valve. Three sets of blood cultures are negative. Further workup is consistent with nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis. Which of the following properties of the vegetations most directly explains why embolization occurred?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The vegetations are composed of organized granulation tissue that fragments under high-pressure aortic flow

choices.label: B
choices.text: The vegetations are densely calcified and brittle, making them prone to fracture with turbulent blood flow

choices.label: C
choices.text: The vegetations are heavily infiltrated with neutrophils, leading to enzymatic degradation and detachment

choices.label: D
choices.text: The vegetations are loosely adherent to the valve surface, making them likely to detach and travel distally

choices.label: E
choices.text: The vegetations are seeded with microorganisms that produce proteases that digest the valve attachment site

explanation.summary: NBTE vegetations embolize because they are loosely adherent to the valves. This makes them likely to fall off and embolize to other parts of the body, including the brain, heart, kidney, and spleen.
explanation.whyCorrect: Vegetations in NBTE have the potential to embolize because they may be loosely adherent to the valves, making them likely to fall off the valves and embolize to other parts of the body. This loose adherence is the key property that predisposes to embolic complications.

whyWrongByChoice.A: NBTE vegetations are composed of sterile fibrin and platelet deposits, not organized granulation tissue; the mechanism of embolization is loose adherence rather than fragmentation of granulation tissue.
whyWrongByChoice.B: NBTE vegetations are bland fibrin-platelet thrombi, not densely calcified structures; calcification is more characteristic of chronic degenerative valve disease.
whyWrongByChoice.C: NBTE vegetations are sterile and do not contain significant neutrophilic infiltrates; neutrophil-mediated destruction is a feature of infective endocarditis.
whyWrongByChoice.E: NBTE vegetations are sterile by definition and do not contain microorganisms; protease production by organisms is a mechanism of valve destruction in infective endocarditis.

questions.stem: A 55-year-old woman presents to the emergency department with sudden-onset right-sided hemiparesis and expressive aphasia. She has no significant past medical history. MRI of the brain reveals an acute ischemic stroke in the left middle cerebral artery territory. Echocardiography shows small vegetations on the mitral valve with no valvular destruction. Blood cultures are negative. CT scan of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis reveals a large pancreatic mass with liver metastases. Which of the following laboratory tests would be most essential to exclude an alternative cause of these vegetations before confirming the diagnosis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Antineutrophil cytoplasmic antibody panel to evaluate for granulomatosis with polyangiitis vasculitis

choices.label: B
choices.text: D-dimer and fibrinogen levels to assess for underlying disseminated intravascular coagulation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Erythrocyte sedimentation rate and C-reactive protein to quantify the systemic inflammatory response

choices.label: D
choices.text: Procalcitonin level and complete blood count with differential to evaluate for occult bacterial infection

choices.label: E
choices.text: Three sets of blood cultures drawn at separate times to exclude culture-negative infective endocarditis

explanation.summary: When vegetations are identified on echocardiography, blood cultures are essential to exclude infective endocarditis, as echocardiography cannot distinguish between infectious and sterile vegetations. Three sets of blood cultures should be obtained to exclude IE before confirming NBTE.
explanation.whyCorrect: Echocardiography cannot distinguish between infectious and sterile vegetations, so blood cultures should be drawn if a vegetation is seen. Three sets of blood cultures are required to exclude infective endocarditis, including culture-negative IE, before the diagnosis of NBTE can be confirmed.

whyWrongByChoice.A: ANCA testing evaluates for vasculitides like granulomatosis with polyangiitis, which is not a common cause of valvular vegetations and would not be the most essential test to exclude an alternative cause.
whyWrongByChoice.B: D-dimer and fibrinogen levels assess for DIC, which may coexist with malignancy but do not help differentiate between infective and noninfective causes of valvular vegetations.
whyWrongByChoice.C: ESR and CRP are nonspecific inflammatory markers that may be elevated in many conditions, including malignancy itself, and cannot reliably distinguish between infective and sterile vegetations.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Procalcitonin and CBC with differential may suggest bacterial infection but are not the standard investigation to exclude infective endocarditis; serial blood cultures are the definitive test.

questions.stem: A 42-year-old woman with systemic lupus erythematosus presents with digital ischemia of her left third and fourth fingers. She also reports a deep, dull ache in her left flank. Urinalysis shows hematuria. A new murmur is heard at the left sternal border. Echocardiography reveals small vegetations on the aortic valve. Blood cultures are negative. ANA and anti-dsDNA antibodies are positive. Which of the following best describes the composition of the vegetations seen in this patient's condition?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dense colonies of gram-positive cocci enmeshed within a fibrin-platelet matrix with surrounding neutrophilic inflammation

choices.label: B
choices.text: Fibrin and platelet deposits containing immune complexes and cellular debris without infectious organisms

choices.label: C
choices.text: Granulomatous inflammation with multinucleated giant cells and central caseous necrosis of the valve tissue

choices.label: D
choices.text: Myxoid stroma with scattered spindle-shaped and stellate cells forming rings and cords within the vegetation

choices.label: E
choices.text: Organized thrombus with extensive neovascularization, hemosiderin-laden macrophages, and fibroblast proliferation

explanation.summary: In SLE-associated NBTE, the vegetations are sterile deposits of fibrin and platelets that contain immune complexes and cellular debris. The mechanism involves immune complex formation, which has been implicated in the pathogenesis of vegetation formation in SLE.
explanation.whyCorrect: In SLE, the mechanism of vegetation formation involves immune complex formation. The vegetations have been shown to contain immune complexes and cellular debris, and they are sterile (containing no infectious organisms). This is consistent with the autoimmune pathophysiology of SLE-associated NBTE.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Dense colonies of gram-positive cocci with neutrophilic inflammation describe the vegetations of infective endocarditis, which is excluded by the negative blood cultures.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Granulomatous inflammation with giant cells and caseous necrosis is characteristic of tuberculosis, not NBTE; valvular vegetations in NBTE are composed of sterile thrombi, not granulomas.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Myxoid stroma with spindle-shaped and stellate cells forming rings and cords describes the histology of cardiac myxoma, not the sterile vegetations of NBTE.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Organized thrombus with neovascularization and hemosiderin-laden macrophages describes a chronic, healing thrombus, not the acute sterile vegetations characteristic of NBTE.

questions.stem: A pathologist is reviewing a series of autopsy hearts with valvular vegetations. She compares the gross and microscopic features of vegetations from three different types of endocarditis. Which of the following combinations correctly distinguishes infective endocarditis from nonbacterial thrombotic endocarditis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Infective endocarditis shows vegetations exclusively on right-sided valves, while NBTE affects only left-sided valves

choices.label: B
choices.text: Infective endocarditis produces large irregular destructive vegetations, while NBTE produces small bland nondestructive vegetations

choices.label: C
choices.text: Infective endocarditis has small nondestructive vegetations, while NBTE has large irregular destructive vegetations

choices.label: D
choices.text: Infective endocarditis vegetations appear on both sides of valve leaflets, while NBTE vegetations are found only along closure lines

choices.label: E
choices.text: Infective endocarditis vegetations consist of bland fibrin deposits, while NBTE vegetations contain bacteria within the thrombus

explanation.summary: Infective endocarditis (IE) is characterized by large, irregular, often destructive vegetations, while NBTE typically manifests with small to medium-sized bland nondestructive vegetations.
explanation.whyCorrect: IE is characterized by large, irregular, often destructive vegetations that can cause significant valve damage. In contrast, NBTE typically presents with small to medium-sized bland nondestructive vegetations. The sterile vegetations of NBTE are rarely destructive to the valve, unlike those of infective endocarditis.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Both IE and NBTE can affect valves on either side of the heart; IE is not exclusive to right-sided valves, and NBTE can occur on any valve.
whyWrongByChoice.C: This reverses the characteristic features; IE has large destructive vegetations, while NBTE has small nondestructive vegetations, not the other way around.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Vegetations appearing on both sides of the valve leaflets is a feature of Libman-Sacks endocarditis (SLE-associated NBTE), not IE; this description does not correctly distinguish IE from NBTE.
whyWrongByChoice.E: This reverses the composition; IE vegetations contain bacteria within a fibrin-platelet matrix, while NBTE vegetations are sterile fibrin deposits without bacteria.

questions.stem: A 51-year-old woman presents with a 3-month history of progressive fatigue, low-grade fevers, and an unintentional 4-kg weight loss. On cardiac auscultation, a sound is heard in early diastole that changes with body position. Echocardiography reveals a pedunculated mass in the left atrium attached to the interatrial septum near the fossa ovalis. Which of the following cytokines is most likely responsible for this patient's constitutional symptoms?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interferon-gamma secreted by tumor-infiltrating cytotoxic T lymphocytes responding to tumor neoantigens

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interleukin-1 beta released from activated macrophages within the myocardial tissue adjacent to the tumor

choices.label: C
choices.text: Interleukin-6 released by the tumor cells causing systemic inflammatory and constitutional manifestations

choices.label: D
choices.text: Tumor necrosis factor-alpha produced by necrotic tumor cells undergoing ischemic degeneration centrally

choices.label: E
choices.text: Vascular endothelial growth factor secreted by the tumor promoting angiogenesis and vascular permeability

explanation.summary: Cardiac myxomas can cause constitutional symptoms including fatigue, fever, weight loss, malaise, arthralgias, clubbing, and Raynaud phenomenon. These are thought to result from the release of interleukin-6 (IL-6) by the tumor cells.
explanation.whyCorrect: The constitutional symptoms of cardiac myxoma (fatigue, fever, weight loss, malaise, arthralgias, clubbing, Raynaud phenomenon) are thought to result from the release of interleukin-6 by the tumor cells. IL-6 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine that mediates the systemic inflammatory response.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Interferon-gamma from cytotoxic T lymphocytes is involved in cell-mediated immunity against infections and some cancers, but is not the cytokine specifically implicated in the constitutional symptoms of cardiac myxoma.
whyWrongByChoice.B: IL-1 beta from activated macrophages is a pro-inflammatory cytokine, but the source described in the context of myxoma constitutional symptoms is IL-6 released specifically by the tumor cells themselves.
whyWrongByChoice.D: TNF-alpha can cause constitutional symptoms in various malignancies, but the specific cytokine implicated in cardiac myxoma constitutional symptoms is IL-6 released by the myxoma cells.
whyWrongByChoice.E: VEGF is indeed secreted by myxomas and promotes angiogenesis within the tumor, but it is responsible for tumor vascularization rather than the constitutional symptoms of fever, fatigue, and weight loss.

questions.stem: A 2-week-old neonate is evaluated for poor feeding and tachypnea. Prenatal ultrasound at 22 weeks showed multiple intracardiac masses. Postnatal echocardiography confirms multiple well-circumscribed, homogeneous, hyperechogenic masses within both ventricles causing partial outflow tract obstruction. Physical examination also reveals hypopigmented macules on the trunk. Genetic testing reveals a mutation in the TSC2 gene. Which of the following proteins is encoded by this gene?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Fibrillin-1, a structural glycoprotein essential for the formation of elastic fibers in connective tissue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hamartin, a tumor suppressor protein that forms a complex to inhibit the mTOR signaling pathway

choices.label: C
choices.text: Merlin, a tumor suppressor protein that regulates contact inhibition and cell proliferation signaling

choices.label: D
choices.text: Neurofibromin, a GTPase-activating protein that negatively regulates the RAS-MAPK signaling cascade

choices.label: E
choices.text: Tuberin, a tumor suppressor protein that functions in a complex to regulate the mTOR pathway

explanation.summary: TSC2 encodes the protein tuberin, a tumor suppressor. Together with hamartin (encoded by TSC1), tuberin forms a complex that regulates the mTOR pathway. Loss of function of either protein predisposes to tumor formation in tuberous sclerosis.
explanation.whyCorrect: TSC2 on chromosome 16 codes for the protein tuberin. Tuberin is a tumor suppressor that, along with hamartin (encoded by TSC1), functions to regulate cell growth. Loss of tuberin function predisposes to the hamartomatous tumors seen in tuberous sclerosis, including cardiac rhabdomyomas.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Fibrillin-1 is encoded by FBN1 and is associated with Marfan syndrome, not tuberous sclerosis; it is a structural connective tissue protein, not a tumor suppressor.
whyWrongByChoice.B: Hamartin is the tumor suppressor encoded by TSC1 on chromosome 9, not TSC2; although both are involved in tuberous sclerosis, the question specifically asks about the TSC2 gene product.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Merlin is encoded by the NF2 gene and is associated with neurofibromatosis type 2, not tuberous sclerosis; it regulates contact inhibition rather than the mTOR pathway.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Neurofibromin is encoded by the NF1 gene and is associated with neurofibromatosis type 1, not tuberous sclerosis; it functions as a RAS-GAP rather than an mTOR pathway regulator.

questions.stem: A 48-year-old woman presents with episodic dizziness and near-syncope that worsen when she is lying on her left side and improve when she sits up. She also reports several weeks of low-grade fever and arthralgias. Echocardiography reveals a 4-cm pedunculated mass in the left atrium prolapsing through the mitral valve during diastole. The mass is surgically excised. Histologic examination shows sparse spindle-shaped and stellate cells with eosinophilic cytoplasm arranged in cords within an abundant lightly stained stroma. Which of the following best describes the stromal composition of this tumor?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Dense type I collagen fibers with scattered fibroblasts and minimal ground substance resembling tendon tissue

choices.label: B
choices.text: Hyaline cartilage matrix with chondrocytes in lacunae surrounded by a territorial matrix of proteoglycans

choices.label: C
choices.text: Loose connective tissue rich in mucopolysaccharides such as chondroitin sulfate and hyaluronan with collagen and elastin

choices.label: D
choices.text: Mature adipose tissue interspersed with thin-walled blood vessels and scattered mast cells within fibrous septa

choices.label: E
choices.text: Sheets of uniform round cells with clear cytoplasm surrounded by a fine capillary network in a chicken-wire pattern

explanation.summary: This is a cardiac myxoma. The myxoid stroma is composed mainly of mucopolysaccharides such as chondroitin sulfate and hyaluronan, along with connective tissue proteins such as collagen and elastin.
explanation.whyCorrect: The 'myxoid' stroma of cardiac myxoma is composed mainly of mucopolysaccharides such as chondroitin sulfate and hyaluronan, along with connective tissue proteins such as collagen and elastin. This gelatinous connective tissue gives the tumor its characteristic appearance and name.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Dense type I collagen fibers with fibroblasts resembling tendon tissue describes dense regular connective tissue or fibrous tumors, not the myxoid stroma characteristic of cardiac myxoma.
whyWrongByChoice.B: Hyaline cartilage matrix with chondrocytes in lacunae describes cartilaginous tissue or chondroma, not the myxoid stroma of a cardiac myxoma.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Mature adipose tissue with thin-walled vessels describes a lipoma, not a myxoma; myxomas are characterized by mucopolysaccharide-rich stroma, not adipose tissue.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Sheets of uniform round cells with clear cytoplasm in a chicken-wire capillary pattern describes oligodendroglioma, a central nervous system tumor, not cardiac myxoma.

questions.stem: A 30-year-old man presents with recurrent episodes of transient ischemic attacks. Echocardiography shows a left atrial mass. He also has diffuse lentigines on his face and trunk, and laboratory studies show elevated cortisol levels with loss of normal diurnal variation. His mother had a similar cardiac tumor resected at age 28. Which of the following is the most likely diagnosis?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Carney complex with familial cardiac myxoma, cutaneous lentigines, and adrenal cortisol-secreting tumor

choices.label: B
choices.text: Li-Fraumeni syndrome with cardiac sarcoma secondary to inherited TP53 tumor suppressor gene mutation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 with cardiac involvement and concurrent pituitary and parathyroid tumors

choices.label: D
choices.text: Sporadic cardiac myxoma with incidental hypercortisolism from an independent adrenal adenoma and lentigines

choices.label: E
choices.text: Tuberous sclerosis complex with cardiac rhabdomyoma, cortical tubers, and cutaneous hyperpigmented lesions

explanation.summary: This patient has Carney complex, a familial disorder involving cardiac myxomas, cutaneous symptoms (lentigines, nevi, cutaneous myxomas), and endocrine tumors. The elevated cortisol suggests an adrenal tumor secreting cortisol leading to Cushing syndrome.
explanation.whyCorrect: Carney complex is the best-known familial disorder associated with cardiac myxomas. It involves a constellation of cutaneous symptoms (lentigines, nevi, cutaneous myxomas) and endocrine tumors that may cause excess hormone secretion. Adrenal tumors may secrete cortisol leading to Cushing syndrome. Familial myxomas often occur in younger patients and may be multiple.

whyWrongByChoice.B: Li-Fraumeni syndrome involves inherited TP53 mutations and predisposes to sarcomas, breast cancer, brain tumors, and leukemia; it is not associated with cardiac myxomas, lentigines, or cortisol-secreting tumors.
whyWrongByChoice.C: MEN1 involves pituitary, parathyroid, and pancreatic tumors but is not associated with cardiac myxomas or cutaneous lentigines.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Sporadic cardiac myxomas account for about 95% of cases but typically occur in older patients, are solitary, and are not associated with lentigines or endocrine tumors; the familial history and young age point to Carney complex.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Tuberous sclerosis is associated with cardiac rhabdomyomas (not myxomas), and its cutaneous findings include hypopigmented ash-leaf spots and facial angiofibromas, not lentigines; it does not cause cortisol-secreting tumors.

questions.stem: A 3-month-old infant presents with poor feeding, tachypnea, and hepatomegaly. Echocardiography reveals a large intracardiac mass in the right ventricle causing significant outflow tract obstruction. The tumor appears homogeneous and hyperechogenic. Biopsy shows enlarged myocytes with clear cytoplasm. Some cells demonstrate thin cytoplasmic strands extending from central nuclei to cell membranes, giving a spider-like appearance. Which of the following best explains the clear cytoplasmic appearance of these tumor cells?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Accumulation of intracellular lipid droplets displacing the nucleus and organelles to the cell periphery

choices.label: B
choices.text: Deposition of amyloid fibrils within the cytoplasm of the myocytes causing cellular expansion and clearing

choices.label: C
choices.text: Mucopolysaccharide accumulation within lysosomes due to a deficiency of lysosomal degradative enzymes

choices.label: D
choices.text: Intracellular glycogen stores that wash away during routine tissue processing leaving clear cytoplasmic spaces

choices.label: E
choices.text: Swelling of mitochondria and endoplasmic reticulum due to irreversible ischemic injury to the cardiac myocytes

explanation.summary: The clear cytoplasm of rhabdomyoma cells is due to large intracellular glycogen stores. During routine tissue processing, the glycogen washes away, leaving clear cytoplasmic spaces and thin cytoplasmic strands (spider cells).
explanation.whyCorrect: On routine staining, the abnormal myocytes of rhabdomyomas are enlarged and have clear cytoplasm secondary to the presence of stored intracellular glycogen. The glycogen stores wash away on routine tissue processing and leave thin cytoplasmic strands behind, which stretch from central nuclei to cell membranes, giving the cells a spider-like appearance.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Intracellular lipid accumulation with peripheral nuclear displacement describes lipid-laden cells such as those in lipomas or fatty change, not the glycogen-rich myocytes of cardiac rhabdomyoma.
whyWrongByChoice.B: Amyloid deposition causes cardiac amyloidosis with restrictive cardiomyopathy, not the well-circumscribed hyperechogenic ventricular masses characteristic of rhabdomyoma.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Lysosomal mucopolysaccharide accumulation describes mucopolysaccharidoses (storage diseases), not the glycogen accumulation seen in rhabdomyomas that washes away during processing.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Mitochondrial and ER swelling from ischemic injury represents hydropic degeneration, not the organized glycogen storage with characteristic spider cell morphology seen in rhabdomyomas.

questions.stem: A 55-year-old woman with breast cancer metastatic to the lungs, liver, and bones undergoes a routine echocardiogram that reveals a mass in the right ventricle. Her oncologist suspects cardiac involvement by her known malignancy. Which of the following facts about cardiac tumors best supports the likelihood that this mass is metastatic rather than a primary cardiac tumor?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cardiac metastases affect up to 10% of patients with metastatic cancer and are far more common than primary cardiac tumors

choices.label: B
choices.text: Cardiac myxomas are the most common primary cardiac tumors and predominantly arise in the right ventricle

choices.label: C
choices.text: Primary cardiac tumors have an incidence of approximately 2% and are predominantly malignant in nature

choices.label: D
choices.text: Right ventricular masses are exclusively secondary tumors because primary cardiac tumors only occur in the atria

choices.label: E
choices.text: The majority of cardiac tumors in adults are rhabdomyomas that typically present as right-sided ventricular masses

explanation.summary: Metastatic (secondary) cardiac tumors are far more common than primary cardiac tumors. Cardiac metastases can affect up to 10% of all patients with metastatic cancer, while primary cardiac tumors have an incidence of only approximately 0.02%.
explanation.whyCorrect: Secondary cardiac tumors from metastases are far more common than primary cardiac tumors. Primary cardiac tumors are rare with an incidence of approximately 0.02%, while cardiac metastases can affect up to 10% of patients with metastatic cancer. Given this patient's widely metastatic breast cancer, a metastatic deposit is far more likely.

whyWrongByChoice.B: Cardiac myxomas predominantly arise in the left atrium (75-80%), not the right ventricle; their predilection for the left atrium near the fossa ovalis is a distinguishing feature.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Primary cardiac tumors have an incidence of approximately 0.02% (not 2%), and the majority are benign, not malignant.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Primary cardiac tumors can occur in any chamber, not exclusively in the atria; familial myxomas may arise in ventricles, and rhabdomyomas most frequently appear in ventricular myocardium.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Rhabdomyomas are the most common cardiac tumors in infants and children, not in adults; in adults, the most common primary cardiac tumor is the myxoma.

questions.stem: A 50-year-old woman presents with progressive dyspnea, orthopnea, and an episode of syncope that occurred when she was bending forward. Echocardiography reveals a 5-cm pedunculated mass in the left atrium attached near the fossa ovalis, prolapsing intermittently through the mitral valve. She is referred for surgical management. Following successful tumor resection, which of the following statements best describes the expected clinical outcome?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Lifelong mTOR inhibitor therapy will be required to prevent recurrence of the tumor after surgical resection

choices.label: B
choices.text: Prognosis is generally very good with rare tumor recurrence after complete surgical resection of sporadic tumors

choices.label: C
choices.text: Radiation therapy to the mediastinum should follow surgery to eliminate any residual microscopic tumor cells

choices.label: D
choices.text: Recurrence rate exceeds 50% within the first year, necessitating scheduled surveillance with annual cardiac MRI

choices.label: E
choices.text: Systemic chemotherapy is recommended postoperatively because of the high rate of distant metastatic spread

explanation.summary: For sporadic cardiac myxomas, surgical resection is the definitive treatment. The prognosis is very good, and recurrence of the tumor is rare after complete resection.
explanation.whyCorrect: Once the diagnosis of sporadic cardiac myxoma is made and surgical resection is performed, the prognosis is generally very good and recurrence of the tumor is rare. Damaged valves or other tissues may require reconstruction, but the overall outcome is favorable.

whyWrongByChoice.A: mTOR inhibitors are used for symptomatic cardiac rhabdomyomas, not myxomas; myxomas are definitively treated with surgical resection without the need for adjuvant medical therapy.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Radiation therapy is not part of the standard management for cardiac myxoma; these are benign tumors that are effectively treated with complete surgical resection alone.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Recurrence of sporadic cardiac myxomas after complete resection is rare, not exceeding 50%; higher recurrence rates are associated with familial myxoma syndromes, not sporadic tumors.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Cardiac myxomas are benign tumors that do not metastasize distally; systemic chemotherapy has no role in their management.

questions.stem: A pregnant woman undergoes a routine fetal ultrasound at 28 weeks' gestation that reveals multiple homogeneous hyperechogenic masses within the fetal cardiac ventricles. Fetal heart rate is 180/min. Amniotic fluid volume is decreased. Genetic testing of the fetus reveals a mutation in TSC1 on chromosome 9. Which of the following clinical findings would you most expect in this child if the associated genetic syndrome manifests fully after birth?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Bilateral acoustic neuromas with progressive sensorineural hearing loss and cafe-au-lait spots on the trunk

choices.label: B
choices.text: Diffuse cutaneous lentigines with pituitary adenoma causing acromegaly and multiple cardiac myxomas

choices.label: C
choices.text: Marfanoid habitus with lens subluxation, arachnodactyly, and aortic root dilation requiring prophylactic surgery

choices.label: D
choices.text: Multiple subcutaneous neurofibromas with axillary freckling and optic pathway gliomas causing visual impairment

choices.label: E
choices.text: Seizures, intellectual disabilities, and facial angiofibromas as part of the classic triad of the associated disorder

explanation.summary: TSC1 mutations cause tuberous sclerosis, which is strongly associated with cardiac rhabdomyomas. The classic triad of tuberous sclerosis is seizures, intellectual disabilities, and facial angiofibromas, although this full triad is found in only a third of cases.
explanation.whyCorrect: Approximately 80-90% of patients with rhabdomyomas have tuberous sclerosis. TSC1 on chromosome 9 codes for hamartin. The classic triad of tuberous sclerosis is seizures, intellectual disabilities, and facial angiofibromas. The fetal tachycardia and oligohydramnios are explained by reduced cardiac output from ventricular obstruction.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Bilateral acoustic neuromas and cafe-au-lait spots describe neurofibromatosis type 2 (NF2), caused by mutations in the NF2 gene encoding merlin, not TSC1.
whyWrongByChoice.B: Cutaneous lentigines, pituitary adenomas, and multiple cardiac myxomas describe Carney complex, which is a different familial syndrome not associated with TSC1 mutations.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Marfanoid habitus, lens subluxation, and aortic root dilation describe Marfan syndrome, caused by FBN1 mutations affecting fibrillin-1, not TSC1.
whyWrongByChoice.D: Subcutaneous neurofibromas, axillary freckling, and optic gliomas describe neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1), caused by mutations in the NF1 gene encoding neurofibromin, not TSC1.

questions.stem: A 6-month-old infant with known tuberous sclerosis is found to have multiple cardiac rhabdomyomas on echocardiography. The largest mass is 3 cm in diameter and is causing intermittent right ventricular outflow tract obstruction with episodes of cyanosis. The parents ask about treatment options. Which of the following is the most appropriate first-line therapy for this symptomatic tumor?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Cardiac transplantation given the multiple tumor burden and high risk of malignant transformation in infancy

choices.label: B
choices.text: Combination chemotherapy with doxorubicin and cyclophosphamide to shrink the rhabdomyoma before surgical resection

choices.label: C
choices.text: Immediate open-heart surgery for complete surgical resection of all rhabdomyomas to prevent cardiac arrest

choices.label: D
choices.text: Medical therapy with an mTOR inhibitor to reduce tumor size and alleviate the obstructive symptoms first

choices.label: E
choices.text: Watchful waiting with serial echocardiographic surveillance since all rhabdomyomas spontaneously regress by age one

explanation.summary: For symptomatic cardiac rhabdomyomas causing arrhythmias or blood flow obstruction, first-line treatment is an mTOR inhibitor to shrink the tumor. Surgical resection is reserved for cases refractory to medical therapy.
explanation.whyCorrect: If rhabdomyomas are large and cause arrhythmias or obstruct blood flow, first-line treatment is an mTOR inhibitor to shrink the tumor. mTOR inhibitors block the mTOR protein activity, regulating cell growth and proliferation. Surgical resection is the next step only if medical therapy is ineffective.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Cardiac transplantation is not first-line for rhabdomyomas; these are benign tumors that often respond to mTOR inhibitors and may spontaneously regress. Rhabdomyomas do not undergo malignant transformation.
whyWrongByChoice.B: Combination chemotherapy is not used for cardiac rhabdomyomas; these are benign hamartomas, not malignant tumors requiring cytotoxic chemotherapy.
whyWrongByChoice.C: Immediate surgical resection is not first-line; medical therapy with mTOR inhibitors should be tried first. Surgery is reserved for tumors refractory to medical treatment.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Watchful waiting alone is inappropriate for a symptomatic tumor causing outflow tract obstruction and cyanosis; while many asymptomatic rhabdomyomas spontaneously regress, symptomatic tumors require active treatment.

questions.stem: A 45-year-old woman undergoes echocardiography for evaluation of exertional dyspnea and is found to have a left atrial mass. Surgical excision is performed, and gross examination reveals a smooth, pedunculated mass with a layer of adherent thrombus on its surface. The tumor arises from the endocardial lining adjacent to the fossa ovalis. Microscopic examination reveals an angiogenic factor commonly secreted by this type of tumor has contributed to its highly vascularized appearance. Which of the following factors is most likely responsible for this finding?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Basic fibroblast growth factor promoting fibroblast proliferation and extracellular matrix deposition within the tumor

choices.label: B
choices.text: Epidermal growth factor stimulating epithelial cell proliferation and differentiation throughout the tumor stroma

choices.label: C
choices.text: Vascular endothelial growth factor promoting new blood vessel formation and contributing to the tumor's vascularity

choices.label: D
choices.text: Transforming growth factor-beta inducing epithelial-mesenchymal transition and promoting tumor invasiveness locally

choices.label: E
choices.text: Platelet-derived growth factor recruiting pericytes and smooth muscle cells to stabilize newly formed vessel walls

explanation.summary: Cardiac myxomas commonly secrete VEGF (vascular endothelial growth factor), which promotes angiogenesis and makes these tumors highly vascularized.
explanation.whyCorrect: Myxomas commonly secrete the angiogenic factor vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), so these tumors tend to be highly vascularized. VEGF promotes the formation of new blood vessels within the tumor, contributing to its vascular nature.

whyWrongByChoice.A: Basic fibroblast growth factor promotes fibroblast proliferation but is not the specific angiogenic factor identified as commonly secreted by cardiac myxomas in the source material.
whyWrongByChoice.B: Epidermal growth factor stimulates epithelial cell growth and is not the angiogenic factor specifically associated with the high vascularity of cardiac myxomas.
whyWrongByChoice.D: TGF-beta is involved in multiple cellular processes including fibrosis and immune regulation, but it is not the angiogenic factor specifically implicated in the vascularity of cardiac myxomas.
whyWrongByChoice.E: Platelet-derived growth factor helps stabilize blood vessels by recruiting pericytes but is not the primary angiogenic factor identified as secreted by cardiac myxomas.
